demoTitle: Morrow beta.28 terminal experience — real-session demonstration (post-fix, verified)
disclosure: This package documents two real, live Morrow sessions run against the SAME code path: an initial run that (by accident of a stale background service) surfaced a real report-integrity bug, and — after the bug was fixed in PR #36 and merged — a fresh verification run against a freshly-restarted orchestrator built from the fixed source. No screenshots, token counts, tool results, timings, or completion results in this package are fabricated, staged, or mocked. Where a metric could not be captured, it is explicitly marked rather than invented.

integrityHistory.priorAudit: A first attempt at this demo package (docs/demo/beta28-terminal, main @ 19efe1f) failed a pre-commit report-integrity audit: the real exported transcript showed 'Now I have full context. Let me apply all the changes.' repeated 12 times inside '## Final Answer'. That package was never committed — see PR #36 for the root-cause fix (assistant turn-boundary tracking in the orchestrator and CLI event/state model).
integrityHistory.rejectedEvidenceLocation: Preserved privately outside the repository (not committed) as regression history — not presented as product evidence.
integrityHistory.reRunDiscovery: The first re-run attempt against this fixed source ALSO showed the legacy-fallback message ('Reconstructed from a task record with no turn boundaries') — traced to a stale, already-running orchestrator background service (installed at C:\Users\[REDACTED-USER]\AppData\Local\Morrow\app\runtime, started ~36 hours before the fix existed) still serving requests on port 4317. That service was stopped and a fresh orchestrator instance was started directly from this repository's fixed source before re-running the demo task.
integrityHistory.finalVerification: A clean re-run against the correctly-restarted, fixed orchestrator produced a report with '## Final Answer' appearing exactly once, zero repeated preamble text, a bounded '## Intermediate Activity' section for the 7 non-final turns, and a clean '## Recovery Summary' — confirmed by reading the actual exported .md file directly, not just a screenshot.

repository.name: Morrow
repository.versionCaveat: The beta.28 terminal-experience work (PR #35) plus the follow-up turn-boundary fix (PR #36) are both merged to main as of the commit above, but no v0.1.0-beta.28 tag or release has been cut. This package documents merged, pre-release code on main — it does NOT claim a 'beta.28 release' exists.

environment.orchestrator: services/orchestrator run directly via `pnpm start` (tsx src/index.ts) from this repository's fixed source, listening on 127.0.0.1:4317 — NOT the pre-existing installed background service, which was stopped first
environment.shell: PowerShell launching the Morrow CLI via tsx (no build step)

provider.name: DeepSeek
provider.note: Real, live API calls — not a mock or replay. Real token metering was visible in this run (e.g. '170.7k in / 13.9k out / 144.3k cached' on the second task), unlike the first (pre-fix) session where the provider had reported 'unknown (not metered)'.

workspace.setup: Fresh git-initialized directory, registered via `morrow init` against the correctly-restarted fixed orchestrator (a v2 workspace was used for the first, orchestrator-stale attempt and discarded once the staleness was diagnosed).

demoTask.description: Build a three-file browser counter app (index.html, styles.css, app.js) with a verification script, then request one intentional second-pass improvement (dark/light theme toggle with localStorage persistence).

pass1.prompt: Build a small polished counter app: index.html, styles.css, app.js, plus a verify.js that checks the files exist and the buttons are wired up. Increment / Decrement / Reset.
pass1.outcome: Completed cleanly, 6 tool calls / 0 failed. Real token usage: 19.4k in / 3.6k out / 15.5k cached.
pass1.verification: verify.js ran in-session (16/16 checks) and was independently re-run via Bash (exit 0).

pass2.prompt: Add a dark/light mode toggle button that persists the user's choice across reloads via localStorage, and extend verify.js to check for it.
pass2.outcome: Completed after 4 genuine, unstaged propose_patch failures (styles.css x2, app.js, verify.js), each recovered via the real tool.strategy_switch escalation to create_file.
pass2.note_on_totals: The persisted report's header ('Tools: 13 calls / 4 failed') counts rows in the toolCalls table; the live terminal status bar showed a higher running count (19) during the same task. This is a pre-existing counting-scope difference between the live UI's cumulative activity counter and the persisted toolCalls list — unrelated to the turn-boundary fix, not investigated further here.
pass2.tokenUsage: 151,347 in / 10,329 out / 128,768 cached (real, metered by the provider this run)
pass2.source: docs/demo/beta28-terminal/transcript/task-2-dark-mode-toggle-EXPORTED.md — a real /export of this task, checked for the real username string (zero occurrences, no redaction needed).

turnBoundaryFixVerification.method: Read the actual exported .md file directly via file-read tooling (not just a screenshot) immediately after /export, and grepped it programmatically.
turnBoundaryFixVerification.verdict: PASS — matches the exact defect class found in the pre-fix package, now absent.

independentVerification.runBy: Claude, directly via the Bash tool — independent of and separate from Morrow's own in-session verification run

browserVerification.method: Real generated files served over local HTTP (python -m http.server on 127.0.0.1:8940) and opened in a genuine, freshly-profiled Google Chrome window launched directly (not through the browser extension, which cannot reach file:// and was used only for a separate quick interactive pass).
interactionsPerformed: Clicked +Increment four times via real OS-level mouse clicks (SetCursorPos + mouse_event) — counter went 0 -> 4, visible in the captured screenshot.
Clicked the theme toggle via the claude-in-chrome extension's real DOM click events in a separate pass — card visibly changed background/border.
Reloaded the page — theme preference persisted via localStorage; counter value correctly reset to 0 (the app only ever writes the theme key, not the count, to localStorage — expected, not a bug).

exportedReport.redactionsInCopy: None required — checked for the real username string and found zero occurrences in the exported report body.

screenshotAccounting.note: Added post-hoc to make the screenshot count auditable without recomputing hashes. Verified against the committed files, their SHA-256 digests, and on-screen content (project name shown in each screenshot) as of this correction.

byteIdenticalDuplicatePairs.note: 07 is a deliberate re-save of 06's image, kept as a separate numbered file to fill the deck's slide sequence; not a distinct capture event.

11 is byte-identical to 08 (same overlay tail-view at this window size), not merely a visually similar 'identical region' as the original per-file description implied. Confirmed via SHA-256, not just visual inspection.

carriedOverFromDiscardedV2Run.verification: Confirmed by reading both screenshots directly: each shows project name 'Beta28-Terminal-v2' on screen (visible in the header line and the Project field), matching this manifest's own workspace.setup note that a v2 workspace was used for the first, discarded, stale-orchestrator attempt. This independently corroborates the 'carried over' label rather than contradicting it. Note the two files' own mtimes (both within the 01:56-01:57 window, i.e. after the PR #36 merge at 01:54:10) reflect when they were copied into the final package directory, not when the underlying v2-workspace screenshots were originally captured — the on-screen project name, not the file mtime, is what establishes their true provenance.

screenshotAccounting.reconciliation: Prior verbal summaries of this package (outside this file) cited '11 fresh screenshots' and 'nine of seventeen original screenshots carried over or intentionally not recaptured.' The second figure is correct and is reproduced exactly by 5 not-recaptured + 2 carried-over-from-v2 + 2 byte-identical duplicate re-saves = 9. The first figure does not reconcile with the committed files under any consistent definition: the correct count of fresh, visually-distinct captures from the verified post-fix run is 8, not 11. That '11 fresh' figure does not appear anywhere in this repository's committed history and could not be traced to a source file; it should be treated as superseded by this accounting.

screenshots.session: carried over from the pre-fix CLI-side run — boot rendering is client-side UI text, unaffected by the orchestrator-side fix

screenshots.redactionDetail: Windows username in the .morrow report path covered with a black box outlined in red; verified via a fresh 5x-zoom crop after redaction.

screenshots.shows: Real Chrome window, real local HTTP server, counter incremented to 4 via real OS-level clicks, theme toggle visible

screenshotsNotRecaptured.reason: These exercise restart/session-resume and responsive-layout/scrollback rendering — both areas the turn-boundary fix (PR #36) does not touch (it only changes how narration text is attributed to turns and selected as the canonical final answer, not restart persistence or terminal layout/scrollback code). They were already proven working with real screenshots in the original PR #35 demonstration pass and are not re-claimed here as 'affected' evidence.
screenshotsNotRecaptured.instruction_interpretation: Phase 10 asked to regenerate 'affected screenshots' — these five are outside that scope by the definition above.

videoDeliverable.reason: Carried over from the original demo attempt: OBS Studio access was requested and denied by the user; per instructions, no workaround was attempted; the user chose to skip video.

pdfSlideshow.generationMethod: Real HTML deck built from the screenshots/data in this manifest, rendered to PDF via headless Chrome print-to-pdf.

knownLintFindings: `git diff --check` reports trailing-whitespace warnings on ~60 blank lines inside transcript/task-2-dark-mode-toggle-EXPORTED.md. These are 4-space-indented blank lines preserved verbatim inside fenced code blocks in the real /export output. The transcript file is left byte-for-byte as exported (sha256 8b6116cf1eeb921ad0a4bade6b028f84dd4ba472c356a5f7a8adbb864cc220be, matching exportedReport.sha256 above) rather than reformatted, because this package's evidentiary value depends on the file being provably unmodified from the genuine /export. This is a known, intentional characteristic, not an oversight.
unavailableMetrics: Exact per-request token counts broken down by individual API call within a task (provider reports task-level totals only).
The tool-count discrepancy noted in demoTask.pass2.note_on_totals was not root-caused in this session — flagged honestly rather than papered over.
